description_long: This object is a standard-sized cigarette, featuring a cylindrical shape with a white paper-wrapped body and a light brown filter at one end. The filter is clearly demarcated from the rest of the cigarette by a subtle color transition. The opposite end displays a grayish, slightly textured tip, indicating the presence of ash, suggesting it has been partially smoked. The paper exterior appears smooth and matte, with no visible branding, logos, or text. The cigarette is compact and lightweight, typical of those found discarded or in use in urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or near public benches.
description: A white paper-wrapped cigarette with a light brown filter and a gray ash tip, showing no visible branding.
description_view_0: The cigarette is seen from the ash tip, highlighting the gray, ashy end and the cylindrical body tapering away.
description_view_1: This view shows the full length of the cigarette, with the white body, brown filter, and gray ash tip all visible.
description_view_2: The cigarette is viewed from the filter end, emphasizing the clean, light brown filter and the white paper body.
description_view_3: The cigarette is angled to show both the filter and the ashy tip, with the white body in between.
